query: PieterPost complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Terms | PieterPost
Postandporch is rated "Poor" with 2.1 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
What has been most frustrating is the lack of transparency. I have contacted customer service multiple times and have repeatedly been told that the mailbox would “ship next week.” According to my Shop account, a shipping label was created on May 28, but there have been no tracking updates or evidence that the package has actually been shipped.

I understand that supply chain issues, manufacturing delays, and backorders happen. What customers deserve, however, is honest communication and realistic expectations. If an item is going to take weeks or months to arrive, that information should be clearly communicated before purchase, not after the order has been placed. [...] Considering 51 reviews, most reviewers were unhappy with their experience overall. Many customers expressed significant frustration with delayed or undelivered orders, often waiting weeks or even months beyond initial estimates. Reviewers frequently reported poor communication from the company, with emails going unanswered or receiving vague, canned responses about supply issues. The customer serv ice was widely criticized as horrible and unresponsive, making it difficult for people to get updates or resolve issues. Delivery services were also a major point of contention, with many orders marked as shipped but never actually picked up by the carrier, or not arriving at all. Some people were dissatisfied with the product itself, receiving damaged, defective, or incorrect items, or finding [...] damaged, defective, or incorrect items, or finding that parts of their order were missing. Conversely, a small portion of people felt satisfied with the product quality, describing items as well-built, sturdy, and stylish, and appreciated the responsiveness of customer service in some instances.See more

results.title: Complaints about postal services | Consumer Council
results.content:
Consumer Council home page

 Search

Menu

# Complaints about postal services

How to make a complaint about lost, delayed, or damaged mail, what you can expect, and how we can help.

If you are unhappy with the service you have received from a postal services provider such as Royal Mail or a delivery company, you can raise a complaint.

Common issues include your mail getting lost, not receiving notification of an attempted delivery, receiving mail that was meant for someone else, your mail being delayed, or your mail getting damaged.

Where you can go for assistance will depend on whether you were the sender or recipient of the goods in question and which postal operator was used.

## Who you should raise your complaint to [...] ## Making a complaint about a postal service provider

Contact your postal service provider directly if something has gone wrong, following their complaints procedure.

If you are unhappy with their response, or have not received a response within the agreed time frame, we may be able to help.

You can contact us for advice and information about your complaint by using our online complaints form or by telephone, email or letter.

## Other organisations that help with postal services complaints

### POSTRS

POSTRS is an independent body whose role is to resolve disputes between regulated postal operators and their customers.

It covers Royal Mail but only deals with complaints about certain products and services. It does not deal with Post Office complaints. [...] ## Who you should raise your complaint to

Where you can go for assistance will depend on whether you were the sender or recipient of the goods, if it is about online shopping, and which postal operator was used.

 If you have sent a parcel, for example to friends or family, and have experienced a problem, you should make a complaint to the operator
 If you have bought something online, raise the issue with the online retailer.

## Compensation you may be entitled to if you have an issue with a postal operator

You may be entitled to compensation if an item you’ve sent, or are due to receive, is lost, damaged or delayed. The amount of compensation you may be able to claim varies depending on the postal service provider that you used.
